questions.question: What is error handling in programming?
keyPoints: Error handling is the process of identifying and resolving errors in a program.
Error handling is used to prevent the program from crashing or producing unexpected results.

questions.question: What is the difference between try and catch blocks?
keyPoints: The try block contains the code that may throw an exception.
The catch block contains the code that handles the exception.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the finally block?
keyPoints: The finally block contains the code that is executed regardless of whether an exception is thrown or not.
The finally block is used to release resources and clean up the program.

questions.question: What is the difference between checked and unchecked exceptions?
keyPoints: Checked exceptions are exceptions that are checked at compile-time.
Unchecked exceptions are exceptions that are not checked at compile-time.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the throw keyword?
keyPoints: The throw keyword is used to explicitly throw an exception.
The throw keyword is used to signal that an exception has occurred.

questions.question: Explain the concept of error handling in programming.
keyPoints: Error handling is the process of identifying and resolving errors in a program.
Error handling is used to prevent the program from crashing or producing unexpected results.
Error handling involves using try-catch blocks to catch and handle exceptions.

questions.question: Explain the difference between try and catch blocks.
keyPoints: The try block contains the code that may throw an exception.
The catch block contains the code that handles the exception.
The try block is used to enclose the code that may throw an exception, while the catch block is used to handle the exception.

questions.question: Explain the purpose of the finally block.
keyPoints: The finally block contains the code that is executed regardless of whether an exception is thrown or not.
The finally block is used to release resources and clean up the program.
The finally block is used to ensure that the program is in a consistent state after an exception has been thrown.
